items.title: 'It's just bulldozed through': Native title holders allege cultural sites have been destroyed by mining company

children.content: A First Nations group on South Australia's Yorke Peninsula says a mining company has bulldozed areas of cultural significance and has started development without a formal agreement with Traditional Owners, but the company says it has "continuously engaged" with the group.

gallery.altText: Two Aboriginal men in black shirts standing alongside each other with a serious expression to the camera

thumbnail.altText: Two Aboriginal men in black shirts standing alongside each other with a serious expression to the camera

items.title: As flight options shrink, this regional city is hoping for a return to rail

children.content: Trains stopped coming to Mount Gambier decades ago, but with fewer flights, locals want them back.

image.altText: A man wearing a red and brown striped jumper stands underneath a black sign with white writing which reads "Mount Gambier"

items.title: AFL apologises after Butters appeal upheld, umpire abuse conviction quashed

children.content: The AFL appeals board has quashed Zak Butters's conviction for abusive language against umpire Nick Foot, saying a Tribunal member listening in while driving his car for part of the original hearing constitutes a miscarriage of justice.

image.altText: AFL star midfielder Zak Butters.

items.title: Labor's warning over 'timing' of Mount Barker, Murray Bridge commitments

children.content: The Malinauskas government made $300 million in election promises for the cities of Mount Barker and Murray Bridge, but when they will be delivered remains uncertain after the two cities did not elect Labor MPs.

image.altText: A woman wearing red and a man in a suit look at a red sign which reads: Labor's plan for Murray Bridge

items.title: Teen accused of toilet block assault took 'calculated steps', court hears

children.content: Prosecutors say the allegations against a teenage boy accused of sexually assaulting a child in a toilet block are so serious he should face trial in an adult court.

image.altText: A street sign that sayd "Regano Rd", with another reading "Toilet" below it.

items.title: Wine grape grower bakes cupcakes to pay bills as fuel costs bite in 'worst' harvest

children.content: Farmers in the midst of harvest in South Australia are having to get creative to help make ends meet.

gallery.altText: A fair-skinned woman in a red floral dress holds a cupcake on a glass plate in a kitchen.

thumbnail.altText: A fair-skinned woman in a red floral dress holds a cupcake on a glass plate in a kitchen.

items.title: Potato company fined $360,000 after propeller 'severely damaged' employee's arm

children.content: A Southern Mallee Potato company has been convicted and fined $360,000 after a plane propeller struck an employee's arm, leaving him with multiple injuries, including partial finger amputations.

image.altText: A crate of potato's on a farm.

items.title: Blood donation eligibility expands for gay and bisexual men, transgender people

children.content: From today, Australian Red Cross Lifeblood will use gender-neutral assessments for blood and platelets donations.

image.altText: a man sits in a reclinable chair with a bandage on his inner elbow and he squeezes a stress ball

items.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate's renewed defamation claim names Australian YouTuber

children.content: An Adelaide YouTuber has been identified in a renewed defamation lawsuit self-described misogynist Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have lodged with a US court against some of their online critics.

image.altText: Andrew Tate wearing sunglasses walks with brother Tristan

items.title: Octopus species mysteriously disappears from Spencer Gulf

children.content: The once-abundant pale octopus has not been caught in lower Spencer Gulf for more than a year, and what happened to the species remains a mystery.

image.altText: An orange octopus on a blue fishing bin lip

items.title: 'No rush': Crows won't put timeline on Dawson's return to play

children.content: Adelaide coach Matthew Nicks says there will be no rush for captain Jordan Dawson to return to playing after the death of his brother last week.

image.altText: The Adelaide Oval big screen displays a picture of the Dawson family with a message of support as the crowd applauds

items.title: A blunder in Narungga leaves SA's electoral authority in damage control

children.content: The 2026 state election has been a reputational disaster for South Australia's electoral authority. Its blunder in Narungga was the "cherry on top".

image.altText: A hand puts a lower house vote into a sealed ballot box as another person points to the top of the box

items.title: 'Pretty invisible': The rise of ADHD medication being prescribed to young women

children.content: Within the sharp increase of ADHD rates in Australia lies a hidden story — more women are being prescribed medication than men. Understanding why is more complicated.

image.altText: A graphic image with silhouettes of men and women's side profiles overlaid with medication and two upwards arrows.

items.title: US singer D4vd charged with murdering 14yo girl found in his Tesla

children.content: The 21-year-old, who rose to fame after going viral on TikTok, is facing charges including first-degree murder, lewd and lascivious acts with an individual under 14, and mutilating a body.

image.altText: A man sits on a couch wearing a dark bandana, vlack shorts, a yellow mesh top and sunglasses.

items.title: For the first time in decades, cash use in Australia has increased

children.content: A new report has found cash use has dropped by more than 50 per cent in almost 20 years. But there has been a slight increase in cash payments since 2022.

items.title: Japan warns of increased risk of mega-quake after 7.7-magnitude one

children.content: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 7.7 struck off the north-eastern coast of Japan, triggering tsunami warnings and widespread evacuation orders affecting about 170,000 people.

image.altText: NHK news report of Japan earthquake and tsunami on April 20th, 2026

items.title: FBI director sues The Atlantic for $348m over report detailing misconduct

children.content: Kash Patel denies all allegations in the report, which quotes numerous sources alleging drinking and absences.

image.altText: A man wearing a cap and jacket emblazoned with the FBI's logo

items.title: Group that helped find Celine Cremer plan their next missing person search

children.content: After helping find missing tourist Celine Cremer, some of the search volunteers plan to formalise their group and take on other missing persons cases, including that of a man who went missing last year.

items.title: Living in an apartment with your dog? Here are four things to consider

children.content: Providing opportunities for your dog to explore, giving them a safe and comfortable space to retreat to — as well as a good routine — are key to keeping your dog happy.

children.content: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

items.title: Disabled NZ parrot uses broken beak to achieve alpha status

children.content: Without a curvy upper beak to get in the way, Bruce the kea uses his lower beak to stab or joust at other birds in his social group.

items.title: Fares are free but some V/Line passengers can't get to work on time

children.content: Some regional commuters hoping to save on petrol are stuck in the slow lane as Victoria's free public transport offer coincides with rail maintenance along the Warrnambool/Geelong line.

image.altText: A man with grey hair and dark rimmed glasses with a white collar over his navy jumper rests his hand on a white picket fence.

items.title: Leave without pay, or lose your job — decision day for smelter workers

children.content: With Australian and international bidders apparently eyeing off the troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter, the workers and their families just want to know they'll have a job at the end of it all.

children.content: Hollywood star Rebel Wilson is being sued in the Federal Court for defamation by one of the lead actor's in her directorial debut film, The Deb.

children.content: Thousands of children across Queensland are sending postcards to former and serving Australian Defence Force members.

items.title: The fiddly and time-consuming reality of driving an EV in Australia

children.content: With no end in sight to soaring fuel prices, 7.30 decided to take an EV for a test drive to see how far it could take our reporter, and if Australia's EV charging network stacks up.

items.title: 'Book early': Australians reconsider holiday and work travel plans

children.content: The war in the Middle East has caused chaos across some of the world's busiest aviation regions, with many Australians now reconsidering their travel plans — or abandoning them altogether.

items.title: Fair Work Commission makes 'historic' fuel order for truck drivers

children.content: The Fair Work Commission orders that retailers, miners and other companies that use truck drivers and transport operators must hold twice-monthly reviews of fuel prices and adjust their rates accordingly.

gallery.altText: A large silver truck drives off into a pink sunset.

thumbnail.altText: A large silver truck drives off into a pink sunset.

children.content: The object formed when a meteorite slammed into Earth more than 800,000 years ago.

items.title: Former footballer Barry Cable found not guilty of sexually abusing child

children.content: Former Australian Rules footballer Barry Cable has been found not guilty of sexually abusing a child in Perth in the 1960s.

items.title: Qld minister says he never broke law amid questions on voting registration

children.content: Queensland's Olympics Minister Tim Mander has insisted he always complied with the state's electoral rules after he enrolled to vote at the address of a staff member.

image.altText: Tim Mander talking and gesticulating to underscore his point
